sections.heading: Reactive Data with Room
sections.content: Room, ViewModel, and Kotlin Flow together form a reactive data pipeline in modern Android apps. Room stores data locally and can emit database changes as a Flow. The ViewModel collects this Flow and exposes it to the UI as StateFlow or LiveData. Whenever the database changes, the new data automatically flows through the ViewModel to the UI without manual refresh logic.

sections.heading: How the Data Pipeline Works
items: Room DAO exposes database queries as Flow<List<Entity>>
Repository receives the Flow and maps database entities to domain models
ViewModel converts the Flow into StateFlow using stateIn()
Fragment or Activity collects the StateFlow safely with lifecycle awareness
Whenever data changes in Room, the UI updates automatically
User actions trigger ViewModel functions that write to the database

rows.description: Observes database tables and emits new values when data changes
Maps database entities into clean domain models
Transforms repository data into UI-friendly state
Collects StateFlow and updates the UI

sections.heading: Room DAO with Flow
sections.content: When a DAO query returns a Flow, Room automatically observes the database table and emits new values whenever the data changes. Unlike suspend functions, Flow provides continuous updates instead of a single result.

sections.heading: DAO Flow Example
items: @Query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = :id") fun getUserById(id: Int): Flow<UserEntity?>
Flow queries automatically emit updates when rows are inserted, updated, or deleted

sections.heading: ViewModel and StateFlow
sections.content: The ViewModel collects the repository Flow and converts it into a StateFlow using stateIn(). StateFlow holds the latest value and survives configuration changes such as screen rotation. The UI simply collects this StateFlow and renders the latest state.

rows.description: map the data into UiState objects
UI collects the StateFlow and updates automatically

sections.heading: Write Operations
items: Insert, update, and delete operations use suspend functions
ViewModel calls repository methods inside viewModelScope.launch
Active Flow queries automatically emit updated data
The UI refreshes automatically without manual reload logic

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Using Room with ViewModel and Flow creates a fully reactive data architecture. Room emits database updates as Flow, the Repository maps and exposes them, the ViewModel converts them to StateFlow, and the UI collects the latest state safely. This approach removes manual refresh logic and ensures that the UI always reflects the latest data stored in the database.
